From the given articles, we find that the dorsal interossei muscles play roles related to movements specific to certain fingers. Specifically, Article-23735 mentions evaluating the second through fifth dorsal interossei by having the patient abduct their 2nd through 5th digits against resistance. Given this information and focusing directly on which fingers have two dorsal interossei (AIIMS May 2019), logically deducing from anatomical descriptions aligns us toward identifying those whose movement patterns match up correctly without redundancy or additional muscular control. By process elimination and direct mention of anatomy in relation to each option, especially since typical human anatomy supports only two dorsal interossei per finger beyond the thumb, the ring finger stands out uniquely fitting criteria consistent throughout discussions about hand functions and assessments thereof.
